$comment: Global economy constants from design doc §8. All first-pass values — tune in Phase 3 against pacing targets.

gifts.$note: design §4: the gift event — unlocked by the first verse's completion, one pile, one yes. pileGoods units of the node's OWN resource (§13 decision: you leave a pile of what the flock likes), spent from camp stock at the node the player chooses; the arrival is deterministic (species) and takes that node as its post. No cost curve — nothing repeatedly buys a creature (the old gatherer/carrier gift curves retired with the kith reversal). pileGoods is a first guess; tune so the third familiar lands ~45-60 min (§2).

hauling.$note: first-guess Phase-1 numbers, not in design doc — a carrier's throughput is baseCarryCapacity/tripSeconds (x carry-capacity upgrades); gathered goods wait in a per-node basket of basketCapacity and overflow is lost. Tune with the loop playtest.

kith.$note: design §4 ladder: six kith slots, four free from minute one; the last two are earned as kithSlot effects (The Old Friend Almanac node, the Warden's Gallery spread). slotsMax is the hard ceiling however many kithSlot effects future content grants.

crafting.$note: first-guess Phase-3 number, not in design doc — seconds per craft at every station before craftSpeedMult upgrades divide it. Tune against the §2 pacing targets (first recipe cooked ~20 min).

tools.$note: flint is knapped (no forge); each later tier also consumes an ingot batch (see recipes)

mastery.$note: curve and xpPerUnit are first guesses, not in the design doc — mastery XP per unit gathered at the node, rung r costs base·growth^r. yieldBonusPerLevel applies to both yield and the raw resource's sell value (§4 '+5% yield/value').

verdure.$note: renownDivisor tuned via the balance model (docs/economy-model.xlsx, ECO-2 Renown) to bank ~10 Verdure at the first fold once Renown-at-fold rose with gatherPerUnit=3. Verdure = floor((lifetimeRenown / renownDivisor) ^ exponent). Model-derived; confirm in playtest.

xp.$note: gatherPerUnit tuned to 3 via the balance model (docs/economy-model.xlsx) so gathering pulls roughly even with familiar time-XP as a Renown source (§9 'every good handled credits Renown') — at 1 it was only ~22% of Renown. Note this also credits warden gathering-skill XP, but recipe gates (max level 5) are already met in minutes at any value, so progression is unaffected. craftPerBatch is still a first guess. Confirm in playtest.

quality.$note: design §5: quality rolls per haul batch (Common 96% / Fine ~3.5% +50% value / Pristine 0.5% base). pristineValueMult is a first guess — the 'windfall' sale isn't priced in the doc. pristineChance = (pristineBaseChance + owned pristineChanceBonus effects) * (1 + tending.pristineChanceBonus while the node's tend window is live) — flat bonuses add points, Tending multiplies (§8).

amber.$note: Amber (design §10): the premium currency's free earn + soft sink. Site finds roll per tick at watchers * digFindsPerHour * digSpeedMult (a separate channel from field sketches — a fully-recorded site keeps surfacing amber; it's old resin with an ancient insect in it, the one thing takeable); a time-skip instantly credits timeSkipHours of full live production for timeSkipCostAmber. All first guesses against the ~40/week-free lean; IAP/ads/weekly cache arrive with the plugin pass.

observation.$note: design §6 (observe · sketch · release). sketch rate = watchers * baseSketchesPerHour * digSpeedMult upgrades * the site's summed unrecorded rarity, rolled per tick; a field sketch is guaranteed once pityTimerHoursWatched hours of watching pass without one. baseSketchesPerHour is a first guess — tune so the first insect plate completes inside the MVP playtest.

tending.$note: burst values are first guesses — tune in Phase 1.

warden.$note: first guess. The warden's own hands at their post (the last-tended node) — always on, boosted by a live burst, straight to camp stock. It's the early-game kickstart (assignment, not a tap surge) AND how a bare node earns its first own-resource replant (design §3), so it must stay positive.

familiarXp.$note: design §4: familiar run XP + Kinship. famXPToLevel(L) = base·growth^L (60·1.12^L), a powerup choice every 5 levels. xpPerSecond = base run XP a stationed familiar earns per second at its post; a wanderer earns half (§2). At Migration run XP converts to Kinship: kinshipGain = floor(√(runXP / kinshipDivisor)); each Kinship level gives +kinshipXpRatePerLevel XP rate and a higher starting level. Levels never scale output. kinshipDivisor tuned to 12000 via the balance model (docs/economy-model.xlsx) — a familiar stationed a whole run banks ~2 Kinship at the first fold; at 1000 it banked ~7. The rest are first guesses; postMatch/comfort multipliers (§8) are a later refinement (todo.md).

replant.$note: design §3: replanting a node's own resource raises its richness — the fourth output lane. replantCost(L) = baseCost·growth^L units of the node's OWN resource, spent from camp stock, per node per run; each level adds richnessPerLevel to the node's base yield (richnessMult = 1 + richnessPerLevel·level). No Renown — it pays back in yield, not the ledger. The warden's post trickle self-funds a virgin node's first replant (§3). Richness resets at Migration (except the Almanac's First Planting — a later slice). All first guesses.
